pattern: If the root has the strictly smallest complexity it can unlock every other computer directly, so any order of the remaining ones works; otherwise some computer can never be unlocked.
hints: Computer 0 is the only initially unlocked machine and must enable the chain, so think about whether it can reach everything.
A computer is unlockable only if some earlier-index machine with smaller complexity is already open; computer 0 covers all of them exactly when it is the unique minimum.
If complexity[0] is strictly smaller than every other value, the count is (n-1)! modulo 1e9+7; otherwise it is 0.

edge_inputs.description: root ties with another value, impossible
root min with duplicate larger values

stress.description: random arrays where the root is forced to be the unique minimum sometimes
